prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for each atom; most-electrophilic/nucleophilic sites identified.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’  → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied furan coordinates (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz); single-point Fukui analysis performed on this fixed geometry (no additional optimisation).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 for the neutral; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states were also computed to obtain f⁺/f⁻.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend).
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations reported as converged.
  Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
    – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – common header present
• Live .out log: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/furan/argo_o3/20260702-222603_furan_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260702-222610.out  (written live during the run; available for inspection).
